TypeSignature.Value: .class public sequential ansi sealed beforefieldinit HBColor extends System.ValueType implements class System.IEquatable`1<valuetype HarfBuzzSharp.HBColor>

Docs.summary: Represents a 32-bit BGRA color value used by HarfBuzz.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
`HBColor` stores a color as a packed 32-bit unsigned integer in BGRA byte order: bits 31–24 are blue, bits 23–16 are green, bits 15–8 are red, and bits 7–0 are alpha. This differs from <xref:SkiaSharp.SKColor>, which uses ARGB byte order (`0xAARRGGBB`).
Use the named component properties (<xref:HarfBuzzSharp.HBColor.Red>, <xref:HarfBuzzSharp.HBColor.Green>, <xref:HarfBuzzSharp.HBColor.Blue>, <xref:HarfBuzzSharp.HBColor.Alpha>) to read individual channels without caring about the packed layout.
When formatted as a string via `ToString`, the color is expressed in `#AARRGGBB` hexadecimal notation.
## Examples
```csharp var red = new HBColor(255, 0, 0, 255); Console.WriteLine(red.Red); // 255 Console.WriteLine(red.Green); // 0 Console.WriteLine(red.Blue); // 0 Console.WriteLine(red.Alpha); // 255 Console.WriteLine(red); // #FFFF0000 ```

MemberSignature.Value: public HBColor (uint value);
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(unsigned int32 value) cil managed

Docs.param: The raw packed BGRA value to store.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct from a raw 32-bit BGRA value.

MemberSignature.Value: public HBColor (byte red, byte green, byte blue, byte alpha);
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(unsigned int8 red, unsigned int8 green, unsigned int8 blue, unsigned int8 alpha) cil managed

Docs.param: The alpha channel value (0–255), where 0 is fully transparent and 255 is fully opaque.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct from individual RGBA channel values.

MemberSignature.Value: public byte Alpha { get; }
.property instance unsigned int8 Alpha

Docs.summary: Gets the alpha component of the color.
Docs.value: The alpha channel value in the range [0, 255], where 0 is fully transparent and 255 is fully opaque.

MemberSignature.Value: public byte Blue { get; }
.property instance unsigned int8 Blue

Docs.summary: Gets the blue component of the color.
Docs.value: The blue channel value in the range [0, 255].

Docs.returns: if the two colors have the same packed value; otherwise, .

Docs.returns: if the specified object is equal to the current color; otherwise, .
